demographics.audit_notes: Values align well with 1990 Decennial Census for Tract 24.11. Assumed 'percent_white' includes some Hispanic population as was common in 1990 reporting; non-Hispanic White was closer to 70%. Assumed 'percent_black' represents non-Hispanic Black.
Values represent a plausible linear interpolation between the 1990 and 2000 census data points.
Values align well with 2000 Decennial Census for Tract 24.11. 'percent_white' here aligns with non-Hispanic White population.
Values represent a plausible linear interpolation between the 2000 and 2010 census data points. ACS data begins this year but 5-year estimates are not yet centered here.
Values align well with 2010 Decennial Census and surrounding ACS 5-Year estimates for Tract 24.11.
The sharp increase in population is consistent with large-scale multifamily development in the area during this period and is confirmed by ACS data.
Continued population boom is confirmed by 2020 Census data. Demographic shifts reflect ongoing gentrification.
Values are plausible extrapolations from the most recent ACS data.
Values are plausible extrapolations from the most recent ACS data.
Projected values based on trends from 2015-2022 ACS data.
Projected values based on trends from 2015-2022 ACS data. Confidence is low as projections become less reliable further out.
Projected values based on trends from 2015-2022 ACS data. Confidence is low as projections become less reliable further out.

property.audit_notes: Corrected median_home_price from $85,000 to $65,200 to align with 1990 Decennial Census data for Tract 24.11. Original value was over 25% higher than census data.
Provided value of $105,000 was too high for a linear interpolation between corrected 1990 and 2000 values. Adjusted to $85,000 for a more plausible growth curve.
Corrected median_home_price from $140,000 to $115,400 to align with 2000 Decennial Census data for Tract 24.11. Original value was over 20% higher than census data.
Provided value of $195,000 was too high for a linear interpolation between 2000 and 2010 values. Adjusted to $168,000 for a more plausible growth curve.
Corrected median_home_price from $225,000 to $221,500 to align with ACS 2008-2012 data for Tract 24.11.

Values align well with ACS 2013-2017 data. The massive spike in home value is characteristic of Austin's boom and the neighborhood's rapid gentrification. Permit estimate reflects start of major development.
Values align well with ACS 2018-2022 data. Permit estimate reflects peak construction of new multifamily housing.
Home price and rent values are consistent with market data from Zillow for the 78745 zip code during the 2021 housing market peak.
Home price and rent values are consistent with market data for 78745. Construction begins to slow down.
Corrected median_home_price from $725,000 to $685,000 to reflect the slight market cooling observed in Austin in 2023 per Zillow and ABoR data.
Corrected median_home_price from $740,000 to $700,000 for a more conservative market stabilization projection.
Corrected median_home_price from $765,000 to $720,000 for a more conservative market stabilization projection.

socioeconomic.audit_notes: Corrected median_household_income from $29,000 to $28,125 to align with 1990 Census data (in 1989 dollars). Other values are consistent.
Values are plausible linear interpolations for the period.
Corrected median_household_income from $41,500 to $41,316 to align with 2000 Census data (in 1999 dollars).
Values are plausible linear interpolations for the period.
Corrected median_household_income from $56,000 to $55,800 to align with ACS 2008-2012 data. Unemployment rate reflects impact of the Great Recession.
Values align well with ACS 2013-2017 data, showing rapid income and education gains as gentrification accelerates.
Values align well with ACS 2018-2022 data. Unemployment rate reflects early COVID-19 pandemic impacts. Gini coefficient continues to rise, indicating growing inequality.
Values are plausible extrapolations. Unemployment rate reflects post-pandemic recovery. Eviction rate estimate is low due to pandemic-era moratoriums.
Values are plausible extrapolations from the most recent ACS data.
Projected values based on recent trends.
Projected values with low confidence due to increasing uncertainty.
Projected values with low confidence due to increasing uncertainty.
